metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add testing ACs covering: (a) unit tests for `compute_validity` failure paths (not-certified, reopened-since, material-changed, each kind); (b) an integration/end-to-end AC that exercises the migrated claim-gate and close-gate against a real (non-mocked) attestation store; (c) explicit removal of retire_attested_pin tests alongside the function deletion.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new `compute_validity` function with at least four distinct code paths (certified+closed+not-reopened+material-match for completion-verifier; material+code-drift+not-reopened for plan-review; and multiple failure modes for each) and migrates two gate call-sites to a new default behavior, yet the plan has zero testing ACs — no unit tests for compute_validity edge cases, no test for the reopen-since path, no 'remove tests for retire_attested_pin', and no end-to-end acceptance criterion for the migrated gate paths that would catch a broken live path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (all sections) — testing work is entirely absent
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] Unit tests cover compute_validity for both kinds, including: not-certified input, signed_at < last_reopened_at, material mismatch, and the happy path for each kind. - [ ] An integration test exercises claim_gate_check and the close gate end-to-end (real HMAC, real attestations map) after migration. - [ ] Tests previously covering retire_attested_pin are removed or updated in this slice.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a RED fixture (parametrized unit test) for `compute_validity` covering: (a) signed_at == last_reopened_at (boundary), (b) signed_at < last_reopened_at (should be invalid), (c) last_reopened_at absent/None (no reopen) — and document the chosen boundary semantics (>= or >) in the ADR.
findings.finding: The `signed_at >= last_reopened_at` boundary condition is asserted as the correctness criterion for reopen-invalidation but no RED fixture or spike is planned to validate the boundary (e.g., same-timestamp edge: should same-second attestation+reopen be valid or invalid?) — this is a behavioral assertion that cannot be settled by reasoning alone.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'completion valid = certified AND closed AND not-reopened-since AND material-unchanged-since signed_at' / compute_validity spec

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rule to `compute_validity`'s spec: when `ticket_state['last_reopened_at']` is `null` (never reopened), the not-reopened-since axis is valid (treat as if last_reopened_at = 0).
findings.finding: The not-reopened-since decision rule `signed_at >= last_reopened_at` has no specified behavior when `last_reopened_at` is `null` (a ticket that has never been reopened). 114f records `last_reopened_at` as 'null if never', but the plan provides no rule for how `compute_validity` handles the null case — whether null means 'always valid on this axis' (the natural semantic) or raises.
findings.location: ## What / AC line: 'completion valid = certified AND closed AND not-reopened-since AND material-unchanged-since `signed_at`'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the relevant AC: 'When `last_reopened_at` is null (ticket never reopened), compute_validity treats this axis as valid (signed_at >= 0 is always true).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC line specifying the gate behavior when `attestations[kind]` is absent (e.g., 'treat as uncertified: return invalid with reason="no attestation for kind"') and ensure compute_validity handles a None/missing attestation input gracefully.
findings.finding: The plan does not enumerate what gates (claim_gate_check, close gate, _child_closure_findings) should do when `attestations[kind]` is absent — key-missing vs. None tolerance is unspecified for consumers of the new attestations map.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'Claim gate verifies+validates plan-review; close + child-closure verify+validate completion-verifier, reading attestations[kind]'
findings.suggested_fix: In compute_validity, check `if attestation is None: return {"valid": False, "reason": "no attestation for kind"}` before any field access, and document this in the AC.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the dependency contradiction: either confirm cc1b is a hard prerequisite (and remove the conditional facade sentence), or demote cc1b to optional and add an explicit AC for facade plumbing in 929e.
findings.finding: The plan lists cc1b as a hard dependency ('Requires … cc1b merged') yet also says '929e plumbs the facade if cc1b has not' — these two statements contradict each other. If cc1b is truly required to be merged first, the conditional facade-plumbing sentence is dead code; if cc1b might not be merged, the dependency declaration is wrong. An implementer cannot know which branch to take.
findings.location: Depends on section / Added (review) paragraph: 'the public rebar.verify_signature facade … 929e plumbs the facade if cc1b has not'
findings.suggested_fix: If cc1b is truly required first: remove 'plumbs the facade if cc1b has not' and replace with 'cc1b is a merge prerequisite; verify facade accepts kind= before starting this slice.' If cc1b might not be merged: promote facade plumbing to a first-class AC and remove the hard dependency claim.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact comparison semantics for the reopen-awareness check (>= vs > for signed_at vs last_reopened_at), note the established JWT iat/invalidated_before prior art, and confirm the chosen operator is correct for the 'reopen-then-immediately-re-sign in the same tick' edge case.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a timestamp-comparison invalidation pattern (signed_at >= last_reopened_at) for a security gate without engaging with the established prior art — JWT iat/invalidated_before, OCSP validity-on-read — where this pattern's boundary condition (>= vs >, same-tick re-sign, clock granularity) is a documented failure mode. The comparison operator and its semantics at the boundary are unspecified.
findings.location: AC line: 'completion valid = certified AND closed AND not-reopened-since AND material-unchanged-since signed_at' / What section: 'compute_validity(attestation, ticket_state, kind, ...) ... signed_at >= last_reopened_at'
findings.suggested_fix: Use strict greater-than (signed_at > last_reopened_at) — matching JWT best practice where a token issued exactly at the invalidation boundary is treated as invalid — and add a comment citing the rationale. If equal-timestamp is intentionally valid (re-sign in the same tick counts as a fresh attestation post-reopen), document that explicitly.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact shape/type of the `attestation` parameter to `compute_validity` (e.g. 'the dict returned by `verify_signature(tid, kind=kind)`' or 'the raw `attestations[kind]` record') and name which field carries `signed_at` for the reopen comparison.
findings.finding: `compute_validity`'s `attestation` parameter type is never defined: the plan says 'the caller runs verify first' and then passes the attestation to `compute_validity`, but the verify_signature result dict (containing `verified`, `verdict`, `manifest`, `signed_at`, etc.) and the raw `attestations[kind]` record dict (containing `manifest`, `algorithm`, `signature`, `key_id`, `signed_at`) have different shapes. An implementer does not know which shape `compute_validity` consumes — the full verdict dict or the raw per-kind record.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC: 'The `attestation` parameter is the dict returned by `verify_signature(ticket_id, kind=kind)` (i.e., includes `verified`, `signed_at`, `manifest`); compute_validity reads `attestation["signed_at"]` for the reopen comparison.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the facade `kind=` ownership: either make 929e unconditionally own the plumb (simplest, idempotent with cc1b) or explicitly add `rebar/__init__.py` to cc1b's scope and make 929e depend on cc1b being merged.
findings.finding: The `rebar.verify_signature` facade `kind=` plumbing has an unimplementable conditional: '929e plumbs the facade if cc1b has not.' At implementation time of 929e, cc1b's merge status is a runtime fact — the plan provides no coordination protocol (e.g., feature flag, import-time check, or explicit dependency ordering) for how the implementer decides whether to add `kind=` to the facade. This is an instruction-locality gap: the decision rule is not co-located with the code change it governs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify compute_validity's behavior when called with a non-certified attestation (assumption violated): either define it as returning `{valid: False, reason: 'not-certified'}` as a defensive guard, or require callers to assert `verified==True` before calling and document this as a precondition error.
findings.finding: `compute_validity` 'ASSUMES the attestation is already HMAC-certified' but no fallback behavior is specified for callers that violate this assumption (e.g., pass a `verified=False` dict). Since `_child_closure_findings` currently wraps signature reads in broad exception handlers and records error strings as verdicts, the new call path `compute_validity(signing.verify_signature(cid, kind=...), ...)` has no specified behavior if `verify_signature` returns a non-certified result that is passed directly to `compute_validity` without a pre-certification check.
findings.location: ## Added (review) — CHILD-CLOSURE call site / AC: `_child_closure_findings` verifies kind='completion-verifier' and runs compute_validity
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the AC: 'As a defensive guard, compute_validity returns {"valid": False, "reason": "not-certified"} if the attestation dict does not carry `verified=True`, even though callers are expected to pre-certify.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan how `compute_validity` evaluates the 'closed' condition for completion-verifier: specify whether it checks `ticket_state['status'] == 'closed'` (which breaks the pre-close gate call in `_completion_precheck`) or whether 'closed' is inferred from the manifest content (e.g. manifest[0] starts with 'completion-verifier:'), and document the pre-close vs post-close call-site distinction so the implementer handles both correctly.
findings.finding: The 'completion valid = certified AND closed' check in compute_validity is mechanically ambiguous at the pre-close gate call site: _completion_precheck calls the verifier BEFORE txn.transition_core writes the STATUS event, so ticket_state['status'] is still 'open'/'in_progress' at that point. If compute_validity checks ticket_state['status'] == 'closed', the close gate's own call will always fail, making the gate non-functional. The plan needs to clarify whether 'closed' is checked via ticket_state at runtime (which breaks the pre-close call) or whether it is a manifest-content check (completion-verifier manifest[0] starts with 'completion-verifier: PASS', which implicitly encodes a successful close verification without re-reading status).
findings.location: AC: 'completion valid = certified AND closed AND not-reopened-since AND material-unchanged-since signed_at' / src/rebar/_commands/transition.py:_completion_precheck (line ~186)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Expand the Scope section to explicitly list docs/adr/ (new ADR file), CLAUDE.md (plan-review-gate note), and rebar/__init__.py (kind= facade) alongside the four src/ files, so the change surface matches the Added ACs.
findings.finding: The Scope section enumerates only four src/ files but the Added (review) ACs require changes to at least three additional files (docs/adr/ entry, CLAUDE.md/plan-review-gate note, and rebar/__init__.py facade). An implementer reading Scope as the authoritative file list would miss these targets.
findings.location: Scope section vs. Acceptance Criteria (added) section
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Scope: 'docs/adr/<slug>.md (new ADR), CLAUDE.md (plan-review-gate note), src/rebar/__init__.py (kind= passthrough if not delivered by cc1b)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a step (or AC) to audit existing docs/CLAUDE.md/ADRs for references to `retire_attested_pin` and update or tombstone them as part of this slice.
findings.finding: The plan removes `retire_attested_pin` but does not audit for stale references to that symbol in existing documentation (e.g., CLAUDE.md, any existing ADRs, or inline code comments), risking stranded references after deletion.
findings.suggested_fix: In the same commit that removes retire_attested_pin, grep docs/ and CLAUDE.md for the symbol name and replace any references with a pointer to the new ADR.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC or implementation note confirming all call sites of `retire_attested_pin` have been audited (e.g., 'grep confirms only transition.py calls this; no public re-export exists') before deletion.
findings.finding: The plan names only transition.py as a caller of `retire_attested_pin` being removed, but does not confirm an exhaustive audit of all callers (tests, other commands, public API re-exports). Removing a function without enumerating all call sites risks leaving broken references that would not surface until runtime or CI.
findings.location: Scope section / What section: 'REMOVE retire_attested_pin (signing.py) and its reopen call (transition.py)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Consider splitting the ADR+CLAUDE.md documentation work and the conditional rebar/__init__.py facade plumbing into a companion cleanup ticket, keeping this slice strictly to the correctness-critical triad: compute_validity + gate migration + retire_attested_pin removal.
findings.finding: As a leaf ticket the plan touches ≥5 source files across ≥4 architectural layers (signing/verification, attest/gate, completion, public facade, documentation), introduces a new interface (compute_validity), and carries 7 acceptance criteria — well above the thresholds that push toward decomposition; the ADR+CLAUDE.md documentation and the rebar/__init__.py facade plumbing are ancillary to the correctness-critical paired-removal and are separable without introducing an invalidation window.
Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.finding: No file_impact declared on a leaf work ticket.
findings.suggested_fix: Record the {path, reason} files this work will touch (e.g. via set_file_impact) so the attestation is scoped to them.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit back-out plan (or expand-contract sequence) for the `retire_attested_pin` removal: describe how to restore mutation-based invalidation if `compute_validity` is found defective post-merge.
findings.finding: The plan removes `retire_attested_pin` and its reopen call as an irreversible destructive step but provides no rollback/back-out plan: if `compute_validity`'s reopen-awareness has a latent bug, the mutation-based safety net is gone with no recovery path described.
findings.location: ## What — 'PAIRED removal (no gap): REMOVE retire_attested_pin ... IN THIS SAME slice'; AC5
findings.suggested_fix: Expand-contract: add compute_validity and migrate gates in one slice; remove retire_attested_pin only after a subsequent observation period or an explicit cut-over commit tagged as the removal point, with rollback instructions in the ADR.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the gate's behavior when `attestations[kind]` is absent on an existing ticket (e.g., explicit 'not-certified' result with a clear reason string, or document that 114f back-fills all records before this slice is deployed).
findings.finding: The gate migration from reading the legacy `signature` field to `attestations[kind]` is a breaking change for any existing ticket records that pre-date 114f; the plan names 114f as a prerequisite but does not describe behavior when `attestations[kind]` is absent (e.g., a ticket created before 114f was merged).
findings.location: ## What — 'Migrate the gates to read attestations[kind]'; ## Depends on

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly justify the `attest.py` → `orchestrator.material_fingerprint` coupling in the ADR or plan (or mitigate by accepting the field set as a parameter to `compute_validity` rather than importing orchestrator directly).
findings.finding: `compute_validity` in `attest.py` is specified to reuse `orchestrator.material_fingerprint` directly, introducing a new cross-component import from the verification layer into the orchestrator module, but this coupling is not explicitly justified or given an interface boundary (e.g., a passed-in callable or a shared constants module).
findings.location: What section: 'it REUSES the existing plan-review material fingerprint (orchestrator.material_fingerprint) as the single source'
findings.suggested_fix: Accept the material field set as an optional parameter (`material_fields=None`) defaulting to `orchestrator.material_fingerprint` at call time, so the coupling is explicit at the call site and testable without orchestrator.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document the concurrency model for gate evaluation (e.g., 'single-writer, file-locked reads guarantee atomicity' or 'ticket_state is loaded once per gate call before any field access') and confirm that attestation + last_reopened_at are read from the same atomic snapshot.
findings.finding: The `signed_at >= last_reopened_at` comparison is a read-time check on two independently-written fields; if a reopen writes `last_reopened_at` concurrently with a gate reading `attestations[kind]` and `last_reopened_at`, the gate can pass on a stale read (TOCTOU), and the plan does not address atomicity or state-isolation for this shared-state access pattern.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria: 'not-reopened-since' / transition.py close gate / compute_validity

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit `rebar.show_ticket(cid, repo_root=repo_root)` call in `_child_closure_findings` (or note that `compute_validity` must tolerate a missing `last_reopened_at` with a defined fallback) to supply the full `child_state` including `last_reopened_at` needed for the not-reopened-since check.
findings.finding: The Added (review) section specifies `compute_validity(signing.verify_signature(cid, kind='completion-verifier'), child_state, 'completion-verifier')` in `_child_closure_findings`, but `child_state` is not available in that function: the loop iterates over `rebar.list_tickets(parent=...)` summaries which are thin dicts (ticket_id, title, status) and do NOT include `last_reopened_at`. A full `rebar.show_ticket(cid)` read is required to obtain `last_reopened_at` for the reopen check — yet the plan does not mention this extra read. Without it, the not-reopened-since condition cannot be evaluated.
findings.location: Added (review) section: '_child_closure_findings verifies kind=completion-verifier ... compute_validity(signing.verify_signature(cid, kind="completion-verifier"), child_state, "completion-verifier")' / src/rebar/llm/completion.py:_child_closure_findings (line 56)
findings.suggested_fix: In `_child_closure_findings`, after confirming `status == 'closed'`, call `child_state = rebar.show_ticket(cid, repo_root=repo_root)` (best-effort, wrapped in try/except) before passing it to `compute_validity`.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
